title: Badge Tailgating and Anti-Passback Violations
dataSources: `index=physical` events with `sourcetype=access_control` from the Access control system where Splunk extracts `event_subtype` (for example tailgate or apb_violation), plus correlating dimensions `door`, `badge_holder`, and `reader_id`; ingest is commonly via Splunk Connect for Syslog (Splunkbase app 4740) or a Heavy Forwarder `inputs.conf` TCP/UDP syslog listener, with optional HEC POSTs to `/services/collector/event` when the head-end exports JSON over HTTPS.
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="access_control"
| where match(event_subtype,"(?i)tailgate|apb|anti.passback")
| stats count by door, badge_holder, reader_id
| sort -count
description: Detects physical-access anomalies where the access control system reports tailgating/piggyback conditions or anti-passback (APB) rule firings—typically `event_subtype` values such as tailgate or `apb_violation` paired with door and `reader_id`—so analysts see follow-through entries without a valid prior exit.
value: Mantrap and data-hall breaches erode perimeter controls, complicate insider-threat and visitor investigations, and weaken evidence for auditors who expect enforced one-person-one-badge rules; prioritizing APB and tailgate signals over routine grants accelerates containment and documents control effectiveness for stakeholders.
implementation: Enable hardened anti-passback on mantrap and high-assurance readers, normalize tailgate/APB exports into `event_subtype` before they reach Splunk, forward with sub-second clock alignment to the controller, and tune alert thresholds per `door` with throttles keyed on `badge_holder` and `reader_id` while routing notifications to physical security operations.
visualization: Sortable Table of violations by `door`, `badge_holder`, and `reader_id`; column Bar chart ranking hot doors; timechart of APB/tailgate counts; single-value KPI for distinct `badge_holder` in the window; optional geofloor or rack-zone overlay when door metadata supplies building and hall tags.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: When `access_control` events are FIELDALIAS-mapped into the Authentication data model (`badge_holder`→user, controller `src`, action inferred from outcomes), enable model acceleration only after cardinality review; tailgate subsets are sparse, so prefer ad-hoc searches on `index=physical` unless leadership-standard tstats dashboards are required.

references.title: Splunk Documentation — Forward data to Splunk Enterprise
Splunk Documentation — Create and manage alerts
Splunk Common Information Model — Authentication dataset

knownFalsePositives: (1) **Elevator vestibule dual-tap workflows** — Some sites require a quick second read at a car-call panel that mimics tailgating; suppress when `reader_id` matches `elevator_dispatch` prefixes in `reader_class.csv` unless `apb_violation` also references the mantrap outer leaf. (2) **Visitor escort overrides** — Guards may hold a door open during escorted tours, producing tailgate telemetry; differentiate using `visit_id` or `escort_flag` fields when exported, or mute during visitor calendar windows tied to ServiceNow change tags. (3) **Controller clock skew during NTP corrections** — Burst tailgate alarms can appear when timestamps jump; pause paging when `controller_time_sync` events occur within ±120 seconds unless two readers agree. (4) **Failover syslog replay** — Secondary controllers may replay historic APB rows after cluster switch; require `count>1` across distinct `_time` buckets or gate on `controller_role=active_primary` markers before reopening incidents.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install Splunk Connect for Syslog (Splunkbase app 4740) or confirm a Heavy Forwarder `inputs.conf` stanza receives syslog/API-forwarded packets from the Access control system head-end; document VLAN, TLS, and secrets handling in the facility runbook per docs/implementation-guide.md.
• Land tailgate and anti-passback exports on `index=physical` with `sourcetype="access_control"`; use `props.conf` transforms so `event_subtype`, `door`, `badge_holder`, and `reader_id` are populated exactly as the detection SPL expects.
• Create an RBAC role such as `physical_security_analyst` with search/filter rights limited to `index=physical`, and isolate compliance exports under a dedicated scheduler identity.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable anti-passback policies on supervised mantraps, map controller alarm codes into `event_subtype=tailgate` or `apb_violation`, and verify a five-minute soak window (`-15m` to `now`) shows non-zero volumes when testers trigger a controlled violation.
• When HTTPS export is required, configure the vendor bridge to POST to `/services/collector/event` with indexed-time skew under one second versus the reader.
• Optional: enrich with visitor or maintenance lookups to later suppress benign tailgate flags.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save the catalog SPL as a report, clone to a scheduled alert on a `cron_schedule` such as `*/5 * * * *` with `dispatch.earliest_time=-5m`, and apply `alert.suppress` on `door` plus `badge_holder` for at least two poll intervals.

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="access_control"
| where match(event_subtype,"(?i)tailgate|apb|anti.passback")
| stats count by door, badge_holder, reader_id
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL
• The base search binds to `index=physical` and `sourcetype="access_control"` so only the Access control system stream is evaluated.
• The `where match(event_subtype,...)` stage isolates tailgate and APB semantics without rewriting vendor codes in multiple OR clauses.
• `stats count by door, badge_holder, reader_id` highlights repeat violators and problematic readers for runbook routing.

Step 3 — Validate
Replay a controller-exported CSV for a known APB test alongside Splunk results in the same interval; reconcile any missing rows with syslog queue depth or HEC acknowledgement failures.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish a dashboard combining the stats table, a timechart of violations, and drilldown to raw events; attach PagerDuty or ticketing actions, record ownership, and rehearse failover when the primary head-end stops streaming.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: During a supervised drill, security triggers an anti-passback violation on mantrap door DH-MANTRAP-01 so the Access control system emits `event_subtype=apb_violation` with populated `badge_holder`, `door`, and `reader_id`; within `dispatch.earliest_time=-15m` the saved search increments the stats row for that tuple and matches the controller live-event console export byte-for-byte on identifiers.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Replay only generic `grant` rows without tailgate or APB codes while temporarily tagging the forwarder `host` with `maintenance_sc4s=true`; the detection should return zero violation rows after the `where match(event_subtype,...)` stage, proving routine access noise is excluded even though volume is nonzero in `index=physical`.

evidence: Retain the scheduled search or dashboard panel output listing `_time`, `door`, `badge_holder`, `reader_id`, and `event_subtype` for the alert window plus the Splunk job SID and a controller screenshot or CSV extract for the same interval.
exclusions: Does not score video-analytics-only tailgating, replace the access control system's authoritative credential database, assess logical identity stores, or certify wiring; mantrap mechanical interlock failures without a syslog code are out of scope unless they map into `access_control` events.
equipmentModels: Physical access control head-end (syslog or HEC export to Splunk)
